title: Pattern Atlas Entry
description: A single entry in the Pattern Atlas catalog — a documented design, architectural, or visual pattern with context, guidance, and relationships.

id.description: Unique slug identifier for the pattern. Used in URLs and cross-references.

name.description: Human-readable display name of the pattern.

aliases.description: Alternative names or synonyms for this pattern.

tagline.description: A one-sentence summary of what the pattern is and does.

description.description: Detailed explanation of the pattern — how it works, when it emerged, key characteristics.

domain.description: The domain or discipline this pattern belongs to.

confidence.description: How established or widely accepted this pattern is.

tags.description: Keywords for search, filtering, and classification.

traits.description: Distinguishing characteristics that define this pattern.

bestFit.description: Situations or contexts where this pattern excels.

avoidIf.description: Conditions or constraints that make this pattern a poor choice.

commonFailures.description: Frequent mistakes or pitfalls when applying this pattern.

contrasts.description: Comparisons with related patterns, clarifying what makes each distinct.

term.description: The name of the related pattern being contrasted.

difference.description: How the related pattern differs from this one.

compatibilities.description: Other pattern IDs that this pattern works well with or is commonly combined with.

promptLanguage.description: Example prompt or instruction template for generating or using this pattern with an LLM.

sources.description: Reference URLs, papers, articles, or documentation for the pattern.

image.description: URL to an illustration or screenshot demonstrating the pattern.

styleRecipe.description: CSS or styling recipe for implementing visual-style patterns.
